How you guys doing? Cool, cool. For those of you guys who are your first time in ClickFunnels, the first time is kind of like weird, but after you play with it a little bit, it gets easier. And soon it's like the coolest, most easy, fun thing in the world. And if you haven't been using it, highly recommend go to funnelfridays.com. Every Friday we do a fun thing. We pick a random product, we make some fun funnels, but you like, we're always showing those things, the more you're watching it and doing it. And all the back archives of our show are there. Like just doing it is the biggest thing. And if you know you're not going to do it or you're scared to do it, highly recommend getting it to our certification program or sitting someone on your team through it. It's for the best way to like really become an actual master or hire one of our certified partners. It's awesome too. So I just think everyone should learn how to do it because it's like the best part of what I do every day is just hanging on a quick funnel. So that's how much fun it is. All right. So I'm going to go probably for like less than 30 minutes because I know that we are all tired and I don't want to just make us all explode. So I just want to kind of end with one core concept. We talk about a lot. I talk about it a lot and I just want to kind of wrap with this because this is how we get people into our funnels. And I just want to make sure that I talk about it a lot, but not many people besides me actually do it. I keep hoping someday that everyone's like, wow, we should do that thing. There's always talks about it because it works really good and he does it and he keeps talking about it. So it's a thing called the dream 100. How many of you guys have heard me talk about it at least 100 times? And you guys have never actually done it yet? Who? Okay, let's come back. So who have heard me talk about the dream 100? Keep your hand up if you actually have done it. You have done it. If you're working on it, I'll give you that. All right. I'll give you that. That's kind of cheating, but you can have it. Okay. So the dream 100 and the dream 100 is basically a concept I learned originally from chat homes. I'm not going to go the whole backstory, but he was using it first off to get the Fortune 500 companies to be his clients. Then he used it to pitch a film, a movie that he wanted to, he wrote the screenplay and he got someone in Hollywood to buy it from him and actually produce it in a movie. And we've been using it for the last seven or eight years to get people to promote our products. I talked to, I think Jill mentioned yesterday, like asking about stuff outside of Facebook. And I look at Facebook as Mark Zuckerberg is one of my dream 100, but that's only one. And if all your business is on Zuckerberg, like if he changes, cause I know what happens when it changes. I've been through this man for 13, 14 years now. So initially my business, my dream 100 was Larry and Sergey from Google and then they screwed me. And then I switched to the other platform, the free Google and then they screwed me. And then it was like my space. And then how do you know who owns that? And then it just disappeared overnight. And then like, it was thing after thing. So I become very like nervous about like having everything in one. How many of you guys business 100% is like all Facebook right now? Okay. So yeah, like two people admit it. There's more than two people. All right. Okay. So those who will win a minute. So remembering just Facebook, Google, whatever, like that's just like one partner. So for me, the way the dream 100 basically works is it comes back to, we talked about day number one. Okay. We got our three markets. You go out to your sub market and then you've created your own niche. So the dream 100 usually is coming one step back. Okay. Which I think is the next slide. So if you've got three markets, you're say you're in wealth, you're in real estate and you've created this, my dream 100, I'm going one step back to the sub market. Cause I want to capture everybody that's in the real estate space or my dream 100. And if I actually created a new market as opposed to just jumping in a niche, there's like 1500 people in. If it's a truly unique thing and a blue ocean, then what's cool about it is all the people that would have been your competitors now can become your greatest partners in the whole world. Okay. So I'm looking at that. And then what I do is very simple and very easy is I make a list. So I say, okay, who are all the list owners that have my potential customers on their list right now? And I make a list of as many people as I can, 20, 20, 30, 40, 100, as many as you can think of. Then who are all the bloggers whose blogs have all my dream customers on it. I make a huge list of all the bloggers. Then who are all the podcasters? Who are all the people in social media influences? Who are the Instagram people, the Facebook people, the Twitter people, all the people that have huge following of my dream customers, people that I want to give me money. Who already has their attention? Okay. And so we do this. We, um, we did a couple of years and kept adding the point. Our dream 100 list is about 500 or 600 people. Recently for the new book launch, we started from scratch, kind of redid this whole exercise, which was about three weeks ago, four weeks ago. And it was really fun. We sat down and we said, okay, who are all the list owners? They have our dream customers. And we had four or five of us in the room. And we do every single person we think of made a list of, I don't know, 40, 50 people. And then we went through bloggers and pie. And so we do, like, I'm not just talking to this. We're doing it. We're still doing it. And then what we're doing for my book launch is, uh, after, after I know who my dream 100 is, there's different ways to get hold of them. It's known, but my favorite thing is we aggressively market them by sending them cool crap in the mail. So this is the box that got shipped out to all of our dream 100 yesterday. Yesterday. And this one out. Who wants to see what's inside of it? You have to promise for my book when the launch is getting that's the only way it works. Okay. So they get this box and they open it up. Cause it's a cool thing. I don't know about you guys, but I'm super busy. I have a million things happening every single day, but the second the UPS two shows up, everybody knows, it's not from the middle of a webinar. They'll interrupt me and bring me the box. Like, I don't know if you guys are the same way. Like it's the only way to get past the gatekeeper directly to me. And if there's somebody and Melanie knows this, everyone knows, like, don't open my boxes. Like that's like Christmas every day. So I order when I order things on Amazon, I order them at different times of the day. Cause I don't want them bumped in one box. I want two boxes. So that's just how I am. And I think most of us are like that, right? So you open it up and there's a letter at the top saying, welcome to the click funnels dream 100. And then I explain like, Hey, I'm Russell. And I talk about this. I talk about this thing is happening and why we're trying to build click funnels and take over the world. And I got this new book launching and by promoting the book, you can sell click funnels. Here's our Dave's and Kevin's contact our Philly managers. We send this cool letter. The letter's cool, but then it's like, if I make a letter and they get it in the mail, no one's going to bring it to their office. No one's going to hand it to them. They're just, it's, it's just going to get piled away. So I have a box full of cool stuff. So we got ours. It's first off, I gave them all a copy of the expert secrets book that's empty. Cause the book hadn't been written yet, but I was like, I got to print out a book and then like just, there's this book I promise is coming out. It's going to be awesome. So this is like, at least tangible and they can like, and they can read the back. So it's like, they know what it's about. And then they're like, Oh, I sent them all a blank copy. I sent the book, which is kind of cool. Yeah. So we sent this out as it started going out. We've already had a couple of people say, Hey, if I promote, but I get a copy of the book early. So I haven't been blank pages. It's actually a very smart strategy because they start to increase curiosity and they're like, well, I want to see what's in there. So I would highly recommend it. If you ever go to a book or a thing, what kind of watch you always send it out with blank first. And they're like, well, what am I going to promote? It was the first time I've ever done that. I wrote the letter and talks about the book, the book and I was like, they don't know what that is. So I mess to some of my team. Like, can we like just print a book with the cover? And anyway, we later we had 200 of them in our warehouse. Then we, so the whole like us versus them finding common enemy throwing rocks. So we like a big believer in that. And then we go in after this big enemy called confusion soft. And it's this whole like comic book telling the story about what like ClickFunnels plans and their goals are. And I am Iron Man. We've got, I'm Thor. Todd got to be Iron Man because he's the smart billionaire and I'm the dumb guy with a hammer. Anyway, so we have the whole story and the end of it is basically telling them like, like, hey, if we're going to defeat this confusion soft, this huge enemy, we need your help. Do you want to come and be part of our dream 100? So it's kind of a fun thing just to like get them to have. And then this thing you talk about and I always want to send weird crap. So when people are other marketing seminars, I was like, did you say weird thing? Russell send them down, which helps really good. We're trying to do something. And then we send them all like a doll of an Avenger, which you got a whole bunch of ones, right? Like some people got Thor, some got Hulk, some got different things. And so that was like the first package in the sequence is going out. Now with our dream 100, we have, we have a monthly schedule of stuff going out to them like this. Because some people are going to get this and they're going to be like, that dude is weird. Why is he like having his own comic book? And they're going to think weird things. But if I am consistent with it, keep getting it, eventually like, who's this dude that keeps sending me stuff? And eventually they're going to contact me and it opens up a whole bunch of doors. So I'm a big believer in sending stuff in the mail to my dream 100. So they're very aware of me at all given times. And so that's a big part of it. As we move to the book launch, we'll be sending out things every single month. Like when we did the dot com secrets book launch, we send the books, people, and then we were giving away my Ferrari. I got on Amazon, I got five or 600 Ferrari keychains. I sent Ferrari keychains to everyone. We had little tiny Ferraris another week, another month we sent a Ferrari to everyone. Like we kept sending them stuff so they're like, I keep getting Ferraris and keychains and all this crap from Rust. Like what is this book he keeps talking about? And it's like being aware, like being in front of them so like they're always there. And like you're like, at least once a month, like they're getting something hand delivered from you from the mailman that they're opening and they're like, this is the cool new thing. So it's a really good way to build relationships. Now in my perfect dream world. Yeah. So you're getting people's physical addresses. Yeah. It's really easy. So email them on Facebook. Hey man, I got a gift for you. What's the best address to send it to? That's it. If you email them, they never respond back. You're doing them. But Facebook is like, hey, we got a gift for you. What's the best place to send it? And everybody except for one person gave us their address. No questions asked. One person is like, well, what do you send to me? So we kicked him off the dream 100. He's out. You don't trust me. You're not going to smell my st... Anyway. So my biggest thing. So after I kind of created my dream 100, there's two, there's two parts of this. The first half of this on the left-hand side is you got to dig your well before you're thirsty. If I just go and message someone, hey, my book's launching. Promote it. That's just not cool. It's not awesome. Relationships come first. So I'm starting this process a long time before I'm asking for anything. So I do that. All these people, I follow all my friend. I'm going to subscribe to them. I comment on their posts on Facebook. I get to know them. I buy their stuff. I'm always trying to figure out how I can serve them. If I had a podcast, which I don't, but if I had a podcast where I interview people, I'd be interviewing them because that gives you a chance to get to know people at a really personal level. I'm trying to figure out how and where I can provide value to my dream 100. Because the coolest thing is if you want one person to dream 100 to say yes, that can be worth a lot of cases, millions of dollars. So I'm trying to think, how can I kiss these guys butts? And I do that in a lot of different ways, but that's what I'm always looking at. People always ask the secret to good marketing and it's like relationships. It's just getting to know people and being cool with them. Even the people that are horrible people will back to you. If you're still cool to them, you never know what's going to happen. In fact, one of my close friends, as we were launching ClickFunnels, I'll say his name, it's Mike Phil saying, one of my closest friends forever, we were about launching ClickFunnels. We were coordinating this whole thing together. I was all excited. And then the launch happened and he didn't respond. And he'd been talking about it a week earlier. And I'm like, hey, man, you there? What's happening? What's happening? Nothing, nothing. And then three days and he's like, hey, turns out I'm partnering with Kyle Graham on 10-Minute Funnels. Looks like we're making competitors, man. Ha, excited for the whatever. And I was like, I was like... I thought you were my best friend and I wanted to stab him and kill him and fly to his house. And eight months earlier, he was going through a really hard time, had him come to my office. I spent all his time with him. My wife was home puking and I did all the stuff for him. And I was just like, oh, I had so much rights to go and want to destroy him. And instead I was like, hey, man, no worries. I totally get it. Good luck. And I just left it and didn't destroy that relationship no matter how bad I wanted to. And three weeks later, he realized that Kyle Graham software sucks and the deal was going to work through and he came back and he said, hey, man, I apologize. I think Kyle didn't work. I was like, no worries, man. That's cooling my help to ClickFunnels. He said, yes, come to my event in three weeks and give a presentation selling your thing at $1,000, which forced him to write a webinar, did the webinar, pitched it there, launched ClickFunnels. He did three webinars to his list without any reciprocation, did over a million dollars in sales, introduced this to about 15 other people. And from that one relationship that I could have screwed up, I probably made, I don't know, $9, $10 million in the last two years off it. So just remember that one person, all of your networks, your Dream 100s are small. There's a lot of connections. And even if people aren't good to you, just be good to them no matter what because you never know what's going to happen. So I'm digging my will, trying to figure out value stuff. And then the next side is how can I work with these guys? So the first level of it is them to promote. That's like my dream. If I get them to send an email, promote, post on Facebook, whatever, that's my biggest dream is to get them to do that. If they won't do that, like I know Tony Robbins is a good friend, no matter what I do, he's never going to promote my book because he doesn't do that kind of thing. But I do know that Tony Robbins is my Dream 100. I know he's got like 1.5 million followers on Facebook and over here. So now I go directly to Zuckerberg. I'm like, hey man, can I promote on Tony's face on his wall? He's like, sure. And then I can buy ads and I can target Tony's audience. So we target all of the audiences in our Dream 100. So step one is getting them to promote. They say, no, cool. We're just going to go directly to your customers through the different channels, through YouTube, through Twitter, through Instagram, wherever we can go. That's the second path. And then the third is like doing like actual true joint ventures. Like how do we integrate together? How do we do cool stuff with those people? Jim Edwards is a good example. He had funnel scripts in his customers. We had ClickFunnels. We did this partnership. We co-created products together and that's the other thing. So that's the Dream 100 strategy and it's the basis of everything we do for our traffic. Everything is tied to that. So nothing external always comes back to that from ads to everything. It's like, how does this come back? Who's our Dream 100? Who already has our customers? And trying to figure out. Like I said, for most of you guys, Zuckerberg's the only dude that's on here. So it's time to start diversifying because I have almost gone bankrupt three times and every single time is because I was focusing on just one thing. One traffic source, one merchant account. Anyway, so one is a scary number. So diversifying and finding these kind of things is big. And if you go through it kind of way, we're showing you like, I don't know if I'm in chat homes seven or eight years ago, we've been doing ever since and it's just fueled our businesses. And all of a sudden, that's how the connections meet. It opens up doors for you to phone calls and all the other kind of stuff. Yeah. Can I tell a story, girl? Please do. I don't have a dream 100, but I have certain people that I really wanted to work with. I'll have one after this meeting. But I used to follow this gal name Heidi Powell, who is huge in the health and fitness industry, and she has a TV show on ABC called Extreme Weight Loss. And I was just like a fan girl and saw her put on a blog. So she would have been in my dream 100, but I just didn't know. Like, I was like, you know, what you just said? She wouldn't have known me. I wouldn't have known her. And I saw her put out a post about this concept, the perfectly imperfect. And it went viral. And it went on Huffington Post. And she said, I'm going to handpick some bloggers to help me promote this campaign. I thought, oh my gosh. Like, I want to help her with that campaign. So I'm like, so stoked. So I went to her website. And I was like, I'm going to email her and tell her. I am your woman. Like, I'm going to help you with this campaign. And I went to her no contact button. There's no way for me to get a hold of her. So I'm like, correct. So I was like, I really, really, really want to help her with this. So I guessed. So I tried like 20 versions of an email address. Heidi at HeidiPal.net, HP at HeidiPal.net, contact. 19 came back. Bounce, bounce, bounce, bounce. One of them worked. And her assistant ran me back. But in the email, what you have to do, you can't just say, hey, I follow you. I want to help you with this campaign. Because now I get a lot like that. What you have to do is you have to put so much. You have to make it one really easy for them to say yes. So it can't be like, make it feel like there's a lot of work for them to do. And secondly, you have to really do a little bit of homework and know them really well and know, understand what their intention and prerogative is and what they're trying to promote and expand on. And then you give them ideas on ways that you can help them with that. And so that is when I created that video that you saw the perfectly imperfect video that went totally viral. And I got over 100,000 new Facebook followers from that one video. Because I was like, I just guessed on an email address. And I had this idea that I wanted to help her. So I do that now. And just FYI, 99 times out of 100, I never hear back from the people on my Dream 100 that I reach out to. And now that I have a following, it gets easier and easier. But especially at the beginning, you have to just know that one will eventually get back to you. And that one could explode like you said, make you $10 million or however much you want. Awesome. Thank you. Thank you. So two kind of things on that. Even us, we'll contact 100 people. And we'll get 30 to say yes. And we'll get six to actually do it. So it's not like I'm batting like 6%. And like 6%, it's like a $3 or $4 million launch. Like it's like awesome, right? So that's part of it. The second thing is what you just said is interesting. And I'll tell you a kind of story behind that. Anyone here know who Telman Knudsen is? Couple of guys. So Telman was a friend. And I met him initially because he called me. I think I was on his Dream 100 back then. He called me. He's like, hey, man, I got this thing I'm creating. It's called List Crescent. And basically, I'm setting up a squeeze page. And then I want you to email your list to my squeeze page. And the people will join my list. And then I will promote your products to your people. I was like, dude, that's the stupidest I've ever heard. He's like, no. I'm going to get 100 people to do this off a huge list. And then I'll promote your stuff. And I was like, you're not going to get 100. I'm not going to say yes to that offer. It's horrible. I kind of made fun of them. I could end it. I was like, what the? Like horrible, right? But I didn't know behind the scenes that Telman called me. He called one person, two, three, four, five, six. And he didn't call them like 48 people. And everybody's told him the same thing, like you're an idiot. And then the 49th person was Kevin Wilkie. He pitched it to Kevin Wilkie. And Kevin's like, huh, that actually might work. He said, shoot, Telman, I'm in. He's like, OK, cool. So they hung up the phone. And they called the next guy and said, hey, I'm doing this project with Kevin Wilkie. Kevin's in and I'm in. And I wanted to do this thing. He said, sure. He said yes, yes. And then he said the next 30 people in the row all said yes. And six months later, I saw this launch where every big name, Joe Vitale, all these people that I looked up through all emailing Telman's squeeze page told them to the list of a couple hundred thousand people and made $1 million in the first 12 months. And I was like, this is insane. So the lesson behind this, how many of you have seen the movie Never Been Kissed? All the ladies raising their hands. I heard about it from somebody I know. But I've never. OK, so there's a scene. So Drew Barrymore is this nerdy girl in high school, Never Been Kissed. That's the name. And then she becomes a reporter. And she becomes this cool reporter. And she's still Never Been Kissed. But then she gets this job from the newspaper, like go back in high school and try to write some articles. So she goes back to high school. And immediately she jumps right back into the nerd clubs and all this stuff. And she's writing these articles. And her boss is like, these are the stupidest. Nobody cares what the chess club is doing. I want articles about what the cool kids are doing. And so she's trying to get in with the cool kids. And nobody will let her in. Then her brother, who's played by David Arquette, he sees that she went back to school. And he was like super jock in high school, who's like a loser now in his life. And he's like, I want to go back to high school too. So he sneaks back into high school. And he gets back in playing the system. And he gets in there. And he starts playing baseball and stuff. And he's watching his sister. And he's like, you got to hang out with the cool kids. And she's like, it's not that easy to be in with the cool kids. He's like, yeah, just watch this. So he's in the cafeteria. He walks over. And he grabs the coleslaw, big old tub of coleslaw. He gets up on the table. And he starts eating the coleslaw, trying to eat the whole thing. And the whole thing is just an entire cafeteria's around him like, oh, yeah, yeah, yeah. And he eats as much he can. He slams the thing down. And suddenly he's the cool kid. And he comes back to her. He says, OK, now watch this. He starts going to all the popular girls. He's like, hey, do you know Josie over here? Like we used to date. But she totally broke up with me. And they're like, really? And like, yeah, she's so cool and whatever. And then she walked away. And then all the girls want to become friends with her. And Dave Arquette says something powerful. He says, all you have to do is get one cool kid to think you're cool, and then you're in. And like, that's the key. Like for Talmud, as soon as Kevin Wilkie said yes, he had one cool kid, and then he was in. And so that's the big thing is like, you may have to ask 100 or 200, like a whole bunch of people. But as soon as you get that one connection in, then it's easier. Like it becomes easier and easier and easier. I said, after Mike Philstein promoted, then he mailed all his friends, hey, Brunson's webinar is killing. You guys should all promote this. Next week I had seven webinars lined up. I was in two or three a day. And then those guys all told there, and it was just like, boom. So that's kind of how the Dream 100 works. And then the biggest thing is like, don't think that you're nobody. Like you're somebody. And as soon as somebody connects with you, then it becomes easy. The other thing that's kind of interesting is I have a lot of people who ask me, hey, what can I do for you? I'm like, I have no idea. I don't know how to answer that question. But people come to me like, hey, Russell, I noticed you in this. I could do this. We want me to do this thing for you. I'm like, awesome. Thank you. Like thinking about that. Also, Sean Stevenson, I had a call with him a while ago. And the way he approached it was really cool as well. He was like, hey, Russell, what are you working on right now? And I was like, oh, we're doing this TV show called Funlac or TV, and da, da, da, da. And I was like, he found what I was excited about. And he was like, oh, that's cool. He's like, how can I support that? And I was like, do you want to be on an episode? He was like, that'd be awesome. And then this instant connection, it was like he asked me first what I cared about. And I told him, and he's like, how can I help be part of that or facilitate that? And then open this huge door. And it was awesome. Trust? So I'm just going to share one of the things that I've done. So as I looked at my Dream 100, there's certain people that kind of feel unreachable. But what I started doing is I said, well, what circles does that person run in to people who are reachable? And so I found kind of the same people that I went to. What I would start doing is I would go to different events or coaching or whatever to one of those people. And when we were done, I would actually say, hey, you know what? Can you make a quick video about so and so? And then I would be on the video and that person would be like, hey, so and so. I just got this quick message, and then I would Facebook it to that person. So what I started doing is associating myself with all these other people that were in that circle. And it started to make me a little bit more well known with those people. I've gotten three videos like that from Travis, by the way. And I've gotten two Dream 100 packages, both which are on my shelf right now. Anyway, he does it good. And the big part about it, too, is don't be like that person who's like, the only reason I'm contacting you is I want something out of them. Like, legitimately, I try to become friends with these people. When all is said and done, we do business people we actually like. Yeah, I'm going to read my book. I don't care. This is like, open up a door, but I'm trying to become friends. The Dream 100 strategy we did with the first book, I didn't know any podcasters at the time. And we sent it to like, 50 or 60 podcasters. And one of them was John Lee Dumas, who like, a week later, messaged me, dude, I read your book. It's freaking amazing. I love it. Like, what have you done with the podcast one year? And like, I was like, all right. I didn't know he knew. I don't think he knew I was before either. It was the connection. And we became friends. I think I've been on the show three or four times now. He's done, Click Phone's a little bit more twice. He's won cars from us. He's made an insane amount of money. And he's never asked for anything in reach. Like, he's one of the coolest guys ever. But it's like, we have a general like, friendship and connection now, but it came off of like, that was the thing that opened the door. And now it's just like, there's so many cool things that happen from that. So it just opens doors, builds relationships, friendships, and it just becomes really cool. So the rule for all you guys, if you're at this next event, you have done something Dream 100 related, which we do. With my kids, I give them swirly's. So I grab them, flip them upside down, and walk in the bathroom. And at first, it was a bad thing. Now they all love it. They're like, give me a swirly. And it's a horrible like, anyway, it's really fun. So maybe swirly's, but we'll let you guys know. Now we have a bathroom. We can do our own swirly's, which would be awesome. So, all right. The last thing I want to end on then, you guys, is just, I know that for all of you guys, you're different spots in your business. And I know we talked about this as kind of our theme for our event in a week and a half from now. But I just want all you guys to remember that, no matter where you're at, if you're beginning, you're in the middle, you're at the end, wherever you're at, whatever your goals are, whatever that result is you want to get, you're legitimately one funnel away from that. And you guys will hear my story in a week and a half of Funalaki Live. My journey has not been like, the whole time. It's been like, and every single time, it was a funnel that turned it around for me. It's not the mechanics of a technical funnel. It's all the stuff we talk about today, the message, the market, the right offer. Like getting people exactly what they want. When you do that and you serve people that level, that's when things take off for you. And so it's, if nothing else, like being consistent with this, being consistent on building funnels, putting your message out there, being consistent with those things. You don't know which ones are gonna hit, which ones are gonna be singles, doubles, which ones are gonna be home runs. But the more of it you're doing, that's how you have success. Okay, the more times you have the bat you have, the more likely you're already hit it out of the park. And with that said, I wanna appreciate you guys for coming to Boise, especially during Super Bowl weekend, and for hanging out, and have a great time, so thank you guys so much! Whoo! Hahaha Thank you. SkipJoNoVenZ
